1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.BLOCK
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.ELSE
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.ELSE_KEYWORD
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.IF
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.LBRACE
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.RBRACE
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.RPAR
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.THEN
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isPartOfComment
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpace
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline
 20→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextSibling
 21→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 23→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 24→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 25→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl
 26→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtBlockExpression
 27→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
 28→
 29→/**
 30→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#formatting-control-flow-statements
 31→ */
 32→public class MultiLineIfElseRule :
 33→    StandardRule(
 34→        id = "multiline-if-else",
 35→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 36→            setOf(
 37→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 38→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 39→            ),
 40→    ) {
 41→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 42→
 43→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 44→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 45→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 46→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 47→        )
 48→    }
 49→
 50→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 51→        node: ASTNode,
 52→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 53→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 54→    ) {
 55→        if (node.elementType == THEN || node.elementType == ELSE) {
 56→            if (node.firstChildNode?.elementType == BLOCK) {
 57→                return
 58→            }
 59→
 60→            if (!node.treePrev.textContains('\n')) {
 61→                if (node.firstChildNode.elementType == IF) {
 62→                    // Allow single line for:
 63→                    // else if (...)
 64→                    return
 65→                }
 66→                if (!node.treeParent.textContains('\n')) {
 67→                    // Allow single line if statements as long as they are really simple (e.g. do not contain newlines)
 68→                    //    if (...) <statement> // no else statement
 69→                    //    if (...) <statement> else <statement>
 70→                    return
 71→                }
 72→            }
 73→
 74→            emit(node.firstChildNode.startOffset, "Missing { ... }", true)
 75→            if (autoCorrect) {
 76→                autocorrect(node)
 77→            }
 78→        }
 79→    }
 80→
 81→    private fun autocorrect(node: ASTNode) {
 82→        val prevLeaves =
 83→            node
 84→                .leaves(forward = false)
 85→                .takeWhile { it.elementType !in listOf(RPAR, ELSE_KEYWORD) }
 86→                .toList()
 87→                .reversed()
 88→        val nextLeaves =
 89→            node
 90→                .leaves(forward = true)
 91→                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.isPartOfComment() }
 92→                .toList()
 93→                .dropLastWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 94→
 95→        prevLeaves
 96→            .firstOrNull()
 97→            .takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
 98→            ?.let {
 99→                (it as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
100→            }
101→        KtBlockExpression(null).apply {
102→            val previousChild = node.firstChildNode
103→            node.replaceChild(node.firstChildNode, this)
104→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(LBRACE, "{"))
105→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)))
106→            prevLeaves
107→                .dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
108→                .forEach(::addChild)
109→            addChild(previousChild)
110→            nextLeaves.forEach(::addChild)
111→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent()))
112→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(RBRACE, "}"))
113→        }
114→
115→        // Make sure else starts on same line as newly inserted right brace
116→        if (node.elementType == THEN) {
117→            node
118→                .nextSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
119→                ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
120→        }
121→    }
122→}
123→
124→public val MULTI_LINE_IF_ELSE_RULE_ID: RuleId = MultiLineIfElseRule().ruleId
125→

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
  8→
  9→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 10→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 11→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 12→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 13→
 14→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 15→
 16→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 17→
 18→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 19→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 20→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 21→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 22→
 23→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 24→
 25→#### Experimental rules
 26→
 27→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 28→
 29→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 30→
 31→#### EditorConfig
 32→
 33→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 34→
 35→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 36→
 37→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 38→
 39→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 40→
 41→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 42→
 43→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 44→
 45→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 46→
 47→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 48→
 49→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 50→
 51→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.

151→#### Custom Ruleset Provider `RuleSetProviderV2`
152→
153→Custom Rule Sets build for older versions of KtLint no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` interface has been replaced with `RuleSetProviderV3`. The accompanying interfaces `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` have been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` respectively.
154→
155→Contrary to `RuleSetProviderV2`, the `RuleSetProviderV3` no longer contains information about the rule set. About information now has to be specified in the new `Rule` class. This allows custom rule set providers to combine rules originating from different rule sets into a new rule set without loosing information about its origin. The typ of the id of the rule set is changed from `String` to `RuleSetId`. 
156→
157→Note that due to renaming and relocation of the `RuleSetProviderV3` interface the name of the service provider in the custom reporter needs to be changed from `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` to `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.ruleset.core.api.RuleSetProviderV3`.
158→
159→The rule id's in `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` have been changed from type `String` to `RuleId`. A `RuleId` has a value that must adhere the convention "`rule-set-id`:`rule-id`". The rule set id `standard` is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Rules created by custom rule set providers and API Consumers should use a prefix other than `standard` to mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.
160→
161→When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the `ruleId` and `about` fields in the wrapped rule, so that it is clear to users whenever they use the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint project.
162→
163→The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. As a result of those changes, the `beforeFirstNode` function in each rule has to changed to something like below: 
164→
165→```kotlin
166→public class SomeRule : Rule(
167→  ruleId = RuleId("some-rule-set:some-rule"),
168→  usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY),
169→) {
170→  private lateinit var myEditorConfigProperty: MyEditorConfigProperty
171→
172→  override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
173→    myEditorConfigProperty = editorConfig[MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY]
174→  }
175→  
176→  ...
177→}
178→```
179→
180→Fields `loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded` and `runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled` have been removed from class `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule` and are replaced with a single field `mode`. The `mode` either contains value `REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED` or `ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED`.
181→
182→#### Custom Reporter Provider `ReporterProvider`
183→
184→Custom Reporters build for older versions of KtLint are no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ReporterProvider` interface has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterProviderV2`. The accompanying interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Reporter` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterV2`.
185→
186→Note that due to renaming and relocation of the `ReporterProviderV2` interface the name of the service provider in the custom reporter needs to be changed from `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ReporterProvider` to `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterProviderV2`.
187→
188→The biggest change in the `ReporterV2` is the replacement of the `LintError` class with `KtlintCliError` class. The latter class now contains a status field which more clearly explains the difference between a lint error which can be autocorrected versus a lint error that actually has been autocorrected.

190→#### Custom rules provided by API Consumer
191→
192→API Consumers provide a set of rules directly to the Ktlint Rule Engine. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule`.

640→Although, Ktlint 0.47.0 falls back on property `disabled_rules` whenever `ktlint_disabled_rules` is not found, this result in a warning message being printed. 
641→
642→#### Default/alternative .editorconfig
643→
644→Parameter "ExperimentalParams.editorConfigPath" is deprecated in favor of the new parameter "ExperimentalParams.editorConfigDefaults". When used in the old implementation this resulted in ignoring all ".editorconfig" files on the path to the file. The new implementation uses properties from the "editorConfigDefaults"parameter only when no ".editorconfig" files on the path to the file supplies this property for the filepath.
645→
646→API consumers can easily create the EditConfigDefaults by calling
647→"EditConfigDefaults.load(path)" or creating it programmatically.
648→
649→#### Reload of `.editorconfig` file
650→
651→Some API Consumers keep a long-running instance of the KtLint engine alive. In case an `.editorconfig` file is changed, which was already loaded into the internal cache of the KtLint engine this change would not be taken into account by KtLint. One way to deal with this, was to clear the entire KtLint cache after each change in an `.editorconfig` file.
652→
653→Now, the API consumer can reload an `.editorconfig`. If the `.editorconfig` with given path is actually found in the cached, it will be replaced with the new value directly. If the file is not yet loaded in the cache, loading will be deferred until the file is actually requested again.
654→
655→Example:
656→```kotlin
657→KtLint.reloadEditorConfigFile("/some/path/to/.editorconfig")
658→```
659→
660→#### Miscellaneous
661→
662→Several methods for which it is unlikely that they are used by API consumers have been marked for removal from the public API in KtLint 0.48.0. Please create an issue in case you have a valid business case to keep such methods in the public API.
663→
664→### Added
665→
666→* Add `format` reporter. This reporter prints a one-line-summary of the formatting status per file. ([#621](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/621)).
667→
668→### Fixed
669→
670→* Fix cli argument "--disabled_rules" ([#1520](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1520)).
671→* A file which contains a single top level declaration of type function does not need to be named after the function but only needs to adhere to the PascalCase convention. `filename` ([#1521](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1521)).
672→* Disable/enable IndentationRule on blocks in middle of file. (`indent`) [#631](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/631)
673→* Allow usage of letters with diacritics in enum values and filenames (`enum-entry-name-case`, `filename`) ([#1530](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1530)).
674→* Fix resolving of Java version when JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS is set ([#1543](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1543))
675→* When a glob is specified then ensure that it matches files in the current directory and not only in subdirectories of the current directory ([#1533](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1533)).
676→* Execute `ktlint` cli on default kotlin extensions only when an (existing) path to a directory is given. ([#917](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/917)).
677→* Invoke callback on `format` function for all errors including errors that are autocorrected ([#1491](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1491))
678→* Merge first line of body expression with function signature only when it fits on the same line `function-signature` ([#1527](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1527))
679→* Add missing whitespace when else is on same line as true condition `multiline-if-else` ([#1560](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1560))
680→* Fix multiline if-statements `multiline-if-else` ([#828](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/828))
681→* Prevent class cast exception on ".editorconfig" property `ktlint_code_style`  ([#1559](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1559))
682→* Handle trailing comma in enums `trailing-comma` ([#1542](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1542))
683→* Allow EOL comment after annotation ([#1539](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1539))
684→* Split rule `trailing-comma` into `trailing-comma-on-call-site` and `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1555](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1555))
685→* Support globs containing directories in the ".editorconfig" supplied via CLI "--editorconfig"  ([#1551](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1551))
686→* Fix indent of when entry with a dot qualified expression instead of simple value when trailing comma is required ([#1519](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1519))
687→* Fix whitespace between trailing comma and arrow in when entry when trailing comma is required ([#1519](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1519))
688→* Prevent false positive in parameter list for which the last value parameter is a destructuring declaration followed by a trailing comma `wrapping` ([#1578](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1578))

692→* Print an error message and return with non-zero exit code when no files are found that match with the globs ([#629](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/629)).
693→* Invoke callback on `format` function for all errors including errors that are autocorrected ([#1491](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1491))
694→* Improve rule `annotation` ([#1574](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1574))
695→* Rename `.editorconfig` property `disabled_rules` to `ktlint_disabled_rules` ([#701](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/701))
696→* Allow file and directory paths in CLI-parameter "--editorconfig" ([#1580](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1580))
697→* Update Kotlin development version to `1.7.20-beta` and Kotlin version to `1.7.10`.

699→* Update Gradle to `7.5.1` version

706→Minor release to address some regressions introduced in 0.46.0

708→### Fixed
709→
710→* Remove experimental flag `-Xuse-k2` as it forces API Consumers to compile their projects with this same flag ([#1506](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1506)).
711→* Account for separating spaces when parsing the disabled rules ([#1508](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1508)).

715→## [0.46.0] - 2022-06-18
716→
717→### Promoting experimental rules to standard 
718→
719→The rules below are promoted from the `experimental` ruleset to the `standard` ruleset.
720→* `annotation`
721→* `annotation-spacing`
722→* `argument-list-wrapping`
723→* `double-colon-spacing`

736→* KtLint disable and enable directives.

739→If your project did not run with the `experimental` ruleset enabled before, you might expect new lint violations to be reported. Please note that rules can be disabled via the the `.editorconfig` in case you do not want the rules to be applied on your project.

741→### API Changes & RuleSet providers
742→
743→If you are not an API user nor a RuleSet provider, then you can safely skip this section. Otherwise, please read below carefully and upgrade your usage of ktlint. In this and coming releases, we are changing and adapting important parts of our API in order to increase maintainability and flexibility for future changes. Please avoid skipping a releases as that will make it harder to migrate.

746→
747→The lint and formatting changes no longer accept parameters of type `Params` but only `ExperimentalParams`. Also, the VisitorProvider parameter has been removed. Because of this, your integration with KtLint breaks. Based on feedback with ktlint 0.45.x, we now prefer to break at compile time instead of trying to keep the interface backwards compatible. Please raise an issue, in case you help to convert to the new API.

 12→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.lexer.KtTokens
 13→import java.io.PrintStream
 14→import java.util.Locale
 15→
 16→public class DumpASTRule
 17→    @JvmOverloads
 18→    constructor(
 19→        private val out: PrintStream = System.err,
 20→        private val color: Boolean = false,
 21→    ) : Rule(
 22→        ruleId = RuleId("$TEST_TOOLING_RULESET_ID:dump-ast"),
 23→        about =
 24→            About(
 25→                maintainer = "Ktlint",
 26→                repositoryUrl = "https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint",
 27→                issueTrackerUrl = "https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues",
 28→            ),
 29→    ) {
 30→        private companion object {
 31→            val ELEMENT_TYPE_SET = ElementType::class.members.map { it.name }.toSet()
 32→        }
 33→
 34→        private var lineNumberColumnLength: Int = 0
 35→        private var lastNode: ASTNode? = null
 36→
 37→        override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 38→            node: ASTNode,
 39→            autoCorrect: Boolean,
 40→            emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, corrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 41→        ) {
 42→            if (node.isRoot()) {
 43→                lineNumberColumnLength = node
 44→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 45→                    .lineNumberOrUnknown()
 46→                    .length
 47→                lastNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 48→            }
 49→            var level = -1
 50→            var parent: ASTNode? = node
 51→            do {
 52→                level++
 53→                parent = parent?.treeParent
 54→            } while (parent != null)
 55→
 56→            out.println(
 57→                (
 58→                    node
 59→                        .lineNumberOrUnknown()
 60→                        .let { String.format("%${lineNumberColumnLength}s: ", it).dim() }
 61→                    ) +
 62→                    "  ".repeat(level).dim() +
 63→                    colorClassName(node.psi.className) +
 64→                    " (".dim() + colorClassName(elementTypeClassName(node.elementType)) + ")".dim() +
 65→                    if (node.getChildren(null).isEmpty()) " \"" + node.text.escape().brighten() + "\"" else "",
 66→            )
 67→            if (lastNode == node) {
 68→                out.println()
 69→                out.println(
 70→                    " ".repeat(lineNumberColumnLength) +
 71→                        "  format: <line_number:> <node.psi::class> (<node.elementType>) \"<node.text>\"".dim(),
 72→                )
 73→                if (node.lineNumberOrUnknown() == "Unknown") {
 74→                    out.println(
 75→                        " ".repeat(lineNumberColumnLength) +
 76→                            "          line_number 'Unknown' is caused by mutations in the AST during formatting".dim(),
 77→                    )
 78→                }
 79→                out.println(
 80→                    " ".repeat(lineNumberColumnLength) +
 81→                        "  legend: ~ = org.jetbrains.kotlin, c.i.p = com.intellij.psi".dim(),
 82→                )
 83→                out.println()
 84→            }
 85→        }
 86→
 87→        private fun ASTNode.lineNumberOrUnknown(): String {
 88→            val lineNumber =
 89→                try {
 90→                    psi
 91→                        .containingFile
 92→                        ?.viewProvider
 93→                        ?.document
 94→                        ?.getLineNumber(this.startOffset)
 95→                        ?.let { it + 1 }
 96→                        ?.toString()
 97→                } catch (e: IndexOutOfBoundsException) {
 98→                    // Due to autocorrect mutations in the AST it can happen that the node's offset becomes invalid. As a result
 99→                    // the line number can not be determined.
100→                    null
101→                }
102→            return lineNumber ?: "Unknown"
103→        }
104→
105→        private fun elementTypeClassName(elementType: IElementType): String {
106→            var name = elementType.toString().substringAfterLast(".").uppercase(Locale.getDefault())
107→            if (name == "FLOAT_CONSTANT" && elementType == KtTokens.FLOAT_LITERAL) {
108→                // resolve KtNodeTypes.FLOAT_CONSTANT vs KtTokens.FLOAT_LITERAL(FLOAT_CONSTANT) conflict
109→                name = "FLOAT_LITERAL"
110→            }
111→            if (KtTokens.KEYWORDS.contains(elementType) || KtTokens.SOFT_KEYWORDS.contains(elementType)) {
112→                name = "${name}_KEYWORD"
113→            }
114→            return if (ELEMENT_TYPE_SET.contains(name)) name else elementType.className + "." + elementType
115→        }
116→
117→        private fun colorClassName(className: String): String {
118→            val name = className.substringAfterLast(".")
119→            return className.substring(0, className.length - name.length).dim() + name
120→        }
121→
122→        private fun String.brighten() = optColor(Color.YELLOW)
123→        private fun String.dim() = optColor(Color.DARK_GRAY)
124→        private fun String.optColor(foreground: Color) = if (color) this.color(foreground) else this
125→
126→        private val Any.className
127→            get() =
128→                this.javaClass.name
129→                    .replace("org.jetbrains.kotlin.", "~.")
130→                    .replace("com.intellij.psi.", "c.i.p.")
131→
132→        private fun String.escape() = this.replace("\\", "\\\\").replace("\n", "\\n").replace("\t", "\\t").replace("\r", "\\r")
133→    }

**Evaluating Kotlin AST inspection approaches**

I'm exploring several ways to print the Kotlin AST for a code snippet, considering adding a temporary test using `DumpASTRule` with `KtLintAssertThat`, writing a short script or main to run it standalone, or leveraging existing CLI tools. Since the AST structure for `else-if` branches is tricky, I want to confirm node types and children precisely. To avoid interfering with current tests, I’m leaning toward a quick, isolated way to print the AST using test tooling or a throwaway Kotlin script. I’m also thinking about how the AST nodes like `ELSE`, `THEN`, and their children like `BLOCK` or `IF` relate, so I can clarify the lint rule’s behavior better. Let’s try to find an optimal way to dump and examine the AST without disrupting the main test suite.

29→

**Planning temporary AST output in tests**

I'm figuring out how to temporarily add a `DumpASTRule` in existing tests to print AST info for debugging. My plan is to modify the first (and maybe second) test to add this rule provider, run tests to get the output, then remove the changes manually afterward to keep the final code clean. This way, I can inspect the AST without overcomplicating by creating new files or permanent changes.

This way, I can confidently identify else-if constructs through structural navigation.

content.text:
Summary:
- Updated `MultiLineIfElseRule` to recognize `else if` branches even when the `if` expression is wrapped by other expressions (Elvis operator, dot/safe-qualified calls, postfix/prefix operators, parentheses). The rule now inspects the PSI for these wrappers and short-circuits whenever the branch ultimately starts with an `if`, so autocorrection no longer rewrites valid `else if` chains.
- Added regression tests in the new `MultiLineIfElseRuleElseIfRegressionTest` to cover the reported scenarios (Elvis operator and chained calls) to ensure they remain lint-free.
- Documented the fix in `CHANGELOG.md` under the Unreleased “Fixed” section.

Tests:
- `./gradlew ktlint-ruleset-standard:test --tests com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.MultiLineIfElseRuleElseIfRegressionTest`

(While running `read_lints`, constraints of the tool produced parse errors for `CHANGELOG.md` because it isn’t Kotlin source; Kotlin files reported no diagnostics.)

 29→/**
 30→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#formatting-control-flow-statements
 31→ */
 32→public class MultiLineIfElseRule :
 33→    StandardRule(
 34→        id = "multiline-if-else",
 35→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 36→            setOf(
 37→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 38→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 39→            ),
 40→    ) {
 41→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 42→
 43→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 44→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 45→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 46→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 47→        )
 48→    }
 49→
 50→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 51→        node: ASTNode,
 52→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 53→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 54→    ) {
 55→        if (node.elementType == THEN || node.elementType == ELSE) {
 56→            if (node.firstChildNode?.elementType == BLOCK) {
 57→                return
 58→            }
 59→
 60→            if (!node.treePrev.textContains('\n')) {
 61→                if (node.firstChildNode.elementType == IF) {
 62→                    // Allow single line for:
 63→                    // else if (...)
 64→                    return
 65→                }
 66→                if (!node.treeParent.textContains('\n')) {
 67→                    // Allow single line if statements as long as they are really simple (e.g. do not contain newlines)
 68→                    //    if (...) <statement> // no else statement
 69→                    //    if (...) <statement> else <statement>
 70→                    return
 71→                }
 72→            }
 73→
 74→            emit(node.firstChildNode.startOffset, "Missing { ... }", true)
 75→            if (autoCorrect) {
 76→                autocorrect(node)
 77→            }
 78→        }
 79→    }
 80→
 81→    private fun autocorrect(node: ASTNode) {
 82→        val prevLeaves =
 83→            node
 84→                .leaves(forward = false)
 85→                .takeWhile { it.elementType !in listOf(RPAR, ELSE_KEYWORD) }
 86→                .toList()
 87→                .reversed()
 88→        val nextLeaves =
 89→            node
 90→                .leaves(forward = true)
 91→                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.isPartOfComment() }
 92→                .toList()
 93→                .dropLastWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 94→
 95→        prevLeaves
 96→            .firstOrNull()
 97→            .takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
 98→            ?.let {
 99→                (it as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
100→            }
101→        KtBlockExpression(null).apply {
102→            val previousChild = node.firstChildNode
103→            node.replaceChild(node.firstChildNode, this)
104→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(LBRACE, "{"))
105→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)))
106→            prevLeaves
107→                .dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
108→                .forEach(::addChild)
109→            addChild(previousChild)
110→            nextLeaves.forEach(::addChild)
111→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent()))
112→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(RBRACE, "}"))
113→        }
114→
115→        // Make sure else starts on same line as newly inserted right brace
116→        if (node.elementType == THEN) {
117→            node
118→                .nextSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
119→                ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
120→        }
121→    }
122→}

124→public val MULTI_LINE_IF_ELSE_RULE_ID: RuleId = MultiLineIfElseRule().ruleId

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
  8→
  9→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 10→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 11→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 12→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 13→
 14→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 15→
 16→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 17→
 18→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 19→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 20→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 21→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 22→
 23→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 24→
 25→#### Experimental rules
 26→
 27→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 28→
 29→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 30→
 31→#### EditorConfig
 32→
 33→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 34→
 35→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 36→
 37→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 38→
 39→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 40→
 41→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 42→
 43→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 44→
 45→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 46→
 47→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 48→
 49→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 50→
 51→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.

151→#### Custom Ruleset Provider `RuleSetProviderV2`
152→
153→Custom Rule Sets build for older versions of KtLint no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` interface has been replaced with `RuleSetProviderV3`. The accompanying interfaces `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` have been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` respectively.
154→
155→Contrary to `RuleSetProviderV2`, the `RuleSetProviderV3` no longer contains information about the rule set. About information now has to be specified in the new `Rule` class. This allows custom rule set providers to combine rules originating from different rule sets into a new rule set without loosing information about its origin. The typ of the id of the rule set is changed from `String` to `RuleSetId`. 
156→
157→Note that due to renaming and relocation of the `RuleSetProviderV3` interface the name of the service provider in the custom reporter needs to be changed from `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` to `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.ruleset.core.api.RuleSetProviderV3`.
158→
159→The rule id's in `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` have been changed from type `String` to `RuleId`. A `RuleId` has a value that must adhere the convention "`rule-set-id`:`rule-id`". The rule set id `standard` is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Rules created by custom rule set providers and API Consumers should use a prefix other than `standard` to mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.
160→
161→When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the `ruleId` and `about` fields in the wrapped rule, so that it is clear to users whenever they use the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint project.
162→
163→The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. As a result of those changes, the `beforeFirstNode` function in each rule has to changed to something like below: 
164→
165→```kotlin
166→public class SomeRule : Rule(
167→  ruleId = RuleId("some-rule-set:some-rule"),
168→  usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY),
169→) {
170→  private lateinit var myEditorConfigProperty: MyEditorConfigProperty
171→
172→  override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
173→    myEditorConfigProperty = editorConfig[MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY]
174→  }
175→  
176→  ...
177→}
178→```
179→
180→Fields `loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded` and `runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled` have been removed from class `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule` and are replaced with a single field `mode`. The `mode` either contains value `REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED` or `ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED`.

182→#### Custom Reporter Provider `ReporterProvider`
183→
184→Custom Reporters build for older versions of KtLint are no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ReporterProvider` interface has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterProviderV2`. The accompanying interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Reporter` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterV2`.
185→
186→Note that due to renaming and relocation of the `ReporterProviderV2` interface the name of the service provider in the custom reporter needs to be changed from `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ReporterProvider` to `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterProviderV2`.
187→
188→The biggest change in the `ReporterV2` is the replacement of the `LintError` class with `KtlintCliError` class. The latter class now contains a status field which more clearly explains the difference between a lint error which can be autocorrected versus a lint error that actually has been autocorrected.
189→
190→#### Custom rules provided by API Consumer
191→
192→API Consumers provide a set of rules directly to the Ktlint Rule Engine. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule`.
193→
194→The type of the rule id's has been changed from type `String` to `RuleId`. A `RuleId` has a value that must adhere the convention "`rule-set-id`:`rule-id`". Rule set id `standard` is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Custom rules created by the API Consumer should use a prefix other than `standard` to clearly mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.
195→
196→Also, the field `About` has been added. This field specifies the name of the maintainer, and the repository url and issue tracker url of the rule.
197→
198→When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the `ruleId` and `about` fields in the wrapped rule, so that it is clear to users whenever they use the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint project.
199→
200→The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. 641→
642→#### Default/alternative .editorconfig
643→
644→Parameter "ExperimentalParams.editorConfigPath" is deprecated in favor of the new parameter "ExperimentalParams.editorConfigDefaults". When used in the old implementation this resulted in ignoring all ".editorconfig" files on the path to the file. The new implementation uses properties from the "editorConfigDefaults"parameter only when no ".editorconfig" files on the path to the file supplies this property for the filepath.
645→
646→API consumers can easily create the EditConfigDefaults by calling
647→"EditConfigDefaults.load(path)" or creating it programmatically.
648→
649→#### Reload of `.editorconfig` file
650→
651→Some API Consumers keep a long-running instance of the KtLint engine alive. In case an `.editorconfig` file is changed, which was already loaded into the internal cache of the KtLint engine this change would not be taken into account by KtLint. One way to deal with this, was to clear the entire KtLint cache after each change in an `.editorconfig` file.
652→
653→Now, the API consumer can reload an `.editorconfig`. If the `.editorconfig` with given path is actually found in the cached, it will be replaced with the new value directly. If the file is not yet loaded in the cache, loading will be deferred until the file is actually requested again.
654→
655→Example:
656→```kotlin
657→KtLint.reloadEditorConfigFile("/some/path/to/.editorconfig")
658→```

717→### Promoting experimental rules to standard 
718→
719→The rules below are promoted from the `experimental` ruleset to the `standard` ruleset.

733→

741→### API Changes & RuleSet providers
742→
743→If you are not an API user nor a RuleSet provider, then you can safely skip this section. Otherwise, please read below carefully and upgrade your usage of ktlint. In this and coming releases, we are changing and adapting important parts of our API in order to increase maintainability and flexibility for future changes. Please avoid skipping a releases as that will make it harder to migrate.
744→
745→#### Lint and formatting functions
746→
747→The lint and formatting changes no longer accept parameters of type `Params` but only `ExperimentalParams`. Also, the VisitorProvider parameter has been removed. Because of this, your integration with KtLint breaks. Based on feedback with ktlint 0.45.x, we now prefer to break at compile time instead of trying to keep the interface backwards compatible. Please raise an issue, in case you help to convert to the new API.

Now, retrieval of unregistered properties results in an exception.
